courses.name: Introduction to Linguistics I
courses.description: Nature of language and linguistic knowledge. Formal description and analysis of language: phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax and semantics.

courses.name: Introduction to Linguistics II
courses.description: Survey of topics in linguistics: language change, sociolinguistics, language acquisition and processing. May include language typology, language contact and writing systems.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 (may be taken concurrently).

courses.name: The Mysteries of Language
courses.description: This course explores some intriguing mysteries of language  - whether it is unique to humans, how children master its complexities so easily, how the brain handles language, how languages are born and die. These questions lead us to interesting discoveries about the human mind.

courses.name: Linguistic Analysis
courses.description: Phonological, morphological and syntactic analysis of linguistic data. Coursework consists primarily of practical exercises in data analysis.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 .

courses.name: Phonetics
courses.description: Description of speech sounds; transcription systems; articulation; acoustics of speech sounds; perception of speech sounds; cross-linguistic diversity and phonetic universals; the role of phonetics in grammar.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 .

courses.name: Language and Communication
courses.description: Some of the central topics in the study of language and communication as pursued by linguists and philosophers. Topics include: the nature of meaning; the connections between language, communication and cognition; language as a social activity.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Communication Differences and Disabilities I
courses.description: A survey course highlighting a variety of communication differences and disabilities. Specific topics vary from year to year but typically will include speech, language, fluency and hearing differences and disabilities.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 and second year standing, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: History of the English Language
courses.description: A historical study of the English language, its structure, variety, and cultural contexts,with an introduction to grammatical terminology and constructions.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Syntax I
courses.description: Introduction to syntactic theory. Representation and analysis of sentence structure, syntactic relations and syntactic dependencies. Testing of grammatical hypotheses.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2005 .

courses.name: Morphology I
courses.description: Introduction to word structure and morphological theory. Topics include inflectional and derivational morphology, morphological processes, and interaction of morphology with phonology and syntax.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2005 and LING 2007 .

courses.name: Phonology I
courses.description: The sound-systems of languages, analysis of phonological structure; generative phonology; phonological rules and derivations; cross-linguistic diversity and universals; segmental phonology; stress; tone.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2001 (no longer offered) or LING 2007 .

courses.name: Special Topic in Linguistics
courses.description: Selected topics in general linguistics not ordinarily treated in the regular course program. Contents of the course vary from year to year.

courses.name: Pragmatics
courses.description: The study of language in its conversational and cultural contexts. Topics include: conversational implicature; deixis; the semantics-pragmatics boundary; speaker's reference; speech acts. May include cross-cultural pragmatics.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing, and one of LING 1001 , PHIL 2001 , PHIL 2504 / COMS 2504 / LING 2504 or PHIL 3506 , or LING 3505 or permission of the Department of Philosophy or School of Linguistics and Language Studies.

courses.name: Semantics
courses.description: Study of language meaning. Lexical meaning and meanings of larger linguistic expressions, including nominal units, verbal units, and sentences. Meaning relationships between utterances. Relationship between linguistic meaning (semantics) and contextual meaning (pragmatics). Basic formal treatments of semantics.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing, and one of LING 1001 , PHIL 2001 , PHIL 2504 / LING 2504 / COMS 2504 or PHIL 3504 / LING 3504 , or permission of the Department of Philosophy or School of Linguistics and Language Studies.

courses.name: Language Processing and the Brain
courses.description: Introduction to adult language processing and neurolinguistics. Psychological processes underlying speech production and perception, word recognition and sentence processing. Biological foundation and neuro-cognitive mechanisms of language. Experimental techniques and methodologies of current psycholinguistic studies.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 or PSYC 2700 and second-year standing, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Child Language
courses.description: Milestones associated with the development of grammatical, pragmatic and metalinguistic competence from birth to about age ten, and the relative contributions of the environment, cognitive development and inborn knowledge to this development.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 and second-year standing, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Communication Differences and Disabilities II
courses.description: An in-depth examination of select topics in the field of communication differences and disabilities. An emphasis is placed on theoretical accounts of specific differences and disabilities and the cross-linguistic evidence for these accounts. Specific topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 and one of ALDS 2604 or LING 2604 .

courses.name: Corpus Linguistics
courses.description: Computer-assisted analysis of electronic collections of naturally occurring language.  Applications in such areas as language variation, grammar, lexicology, phraseology, translation, and learner language.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Sociolinguistics
courses.description: The place of language within society; bilingual and multilingual communities; language, social mobility and social stratification; sociolinguistic factors in language change.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 1001 and third-year standing.

courses.name: Structure of a Specific Language
courses.description: Description and analysis of the structure of a specific language applying phonology, morphology, syntax, and semantics. Language to be studied will be announced in advance by the School.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2001 (no longer offered) or LING 2005 or LING 2007 .

courses.name: Beyond the BA
courses.description: Students explore personal and professional transitions from undergraduate to entering the workforce or graduate school. Topics may include self-assessments, career management skills, and networking. Both academic and practical work, featuring interaction from career specialists, graduate schools, professionals, and employed LING graduates.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in ALDS or LING or permission of the School.

courses.name: Historical Linguistics I
courses.description: Language change; sound change; analogy; the comparative method; internal reconstruction; the philological method; historical linguistics and pre-history; language change and theories of grammar.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2007 .

courses.name: Language Typology and Universals
courses.description: Cross-linguistic survey of syntactic and morphological patterns found in the languages of the world. Typological classification and identification of language universals.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2005 .

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Research under the supervision of a member of the School. Normally available only to third- and fourth-year students in Linguistics.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Research under the supervision of a member of the School. Normally available only to third- and fourth-year students in Linguistics.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Syntax II
courses.description: Advanced topics in syntax.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3004 and third-year standing.

courses.name: Morphology II
courses.description: Advanced topics in morphology.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3005 and third-year standing.

courses.name: Phonology II
courses.description: Advanced topics in phonology.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3007 , and third-year standing.

courses.name: Special Topic in Linguistics
courses.description: Examination of a topic or more specialized area in linguistics or language study. Topic to be announced. Repeatable for credit when the topic changes.
courses.prerequisites: third- or fourth-year standing in Linguistics or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Diversité du français
courses.description: Études des variétés du français, dans ses dimensions spatiales. Le contenu précis de ce cours varie selon les années. Consulter le site web du Département de français pour obtenir les détails. The course is taught in French, but students will submit written assignments in English.
courses.prerequisites: FREN 2401 and FREN 3050 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Diachronie du français
courses.description: Étude du français, dans ses dimensions historiques. Le contenu précis de ce cours varie selon les années. Consulter le site web du Département de français pour obtenir les détails. The course is taught in French, but students will submit written assignments in English.
courses.prerequisites: FREN 2401 and FREN 3050 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Analyse du français
courses.description: Étude du français, dans ses dimensions morphologiques, syntaxiques ou phonologiques. Le contenu précis de ce cours varie selon les années. Consulter le site web du Département de français pour obtenir les détails. Course is taught in French, but students will submit written assignments in English.
courses.prerequisites: FREN 2401 and FREN 3050 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Variation du français
courses.description: Étude des variations internes de la langue, dans des dimensions orales/écrites. Le contenu précis de ce cours varie selon les années. Consulter le site web du Département de français pour obtenir les détails. Course is taught in French, but students submit assignments in English.
courses.prerequisites: FREN 2401 and FREN 3050 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Formal Semantics
courses.description: Advanced topics in compositional semantics and its interfaces. Topics may include: logic, semantic types, lambda calculus, intentional contexts, possible world semantics, interfaces with syntax and pragmatics quantification, anaphora, presupposition, implicatures, scope and binding, and model theory.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3505 or PHIL 3506 , and third-year standing, or permission of the Department of Philosophy or School of Linguistics and Language Studies.

courses.name: Lexical Semantics
courses.description: Study of the meaning of words. Topics may include lexical decomposition, meaning variation, lexical relations, and lexical aspect.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3505 or PHIL 3506 , and third-year standing.

courses.name: Cognitive Neuroscience of Language
courses.description: Further study of psychological and neurolinguistic mechanisms of adult language processing. May include topics from first language acquisition.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3601 or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: First Language Acquisition
courses.description: Advanced topics in language acquisition and development and the relative contributions of the environment, cognitive development, and inborn knowledge.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 and LING 3603 .

courses.name: Practicum in Speech Language Pathology
courses.description: Through field placements, students pursue personal learning objectives related to speech-language pathology, with a focus on the clinical application of knowledge gained in the Psycholinguistics and Communication Differences concentration.
courses.prerequisites: LING 3604 , fourth-year Honours standing in B.A. or B.Sc. in Linguistics with a Concentration in Psycholinguistics and Communication Disorders with a CGPA of 10.0 in the major, and permission from the School of Linguistics and Language Studies.

courses.name: Psycholinguistic Research Methods
courses.description: Experimental methodologies used in current psycholinguistic studies. Topics include experimental design and techniques, descriptive statistics, and interpreting and reporting research findings.
courses.prerequisites: third- or fourth-year standing and LING 3601 , or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Statistics for Language Research
courses.description: Application of statistical procedures to analysis of language data and to problems of measurement in experimental linguistics, applied linguistics, psycholinguistics, and related fields.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing in Linguistics or Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies or Cognitive Science, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Linguistic Field Methods
courses.description: With a language consultant, students discover the phonological, morphological, and syntactic structures of the target language using linguistic elicitation. Language will vary from year to year, but will normally be a non-European language.  Language documentation, data management, ethical issues surrounding research in Indigenous communities.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2005 and LING 2007 .

courses.name: Historical Linguistics: English
courses.description: A theory-intensive course that will study the development of English starting with Proto-Indo-European progressing through Common Germanic to the stages of English itself. Topics include phonological sound changes, phonemic inventories, and morphological and syntactic typology.
courses.prerequisites: LING 2005 and LING 2007 , and one of LING 3005 , LING 3810 or LING 3811 .

courses.name: Old English
courses.description: Studies in Old English literature and its cultural and historical contexts. Instruction in grammar to facilitate reading knowledge of the Old English language.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Independent Study in Linguistics
courses.description: Permits fourth-year Honours students to pursue their interests in a selected area of linguistics.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Independent Study in Linguistics
courses.description: Permits fourth-year Honours students to pursue their interests in a selected area of linguistics.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Honours Project in Experimental Linguistics
courses.description: Students choose existing study in linguistic literature, replicate the study, present findings, compare to original study. Practical experience gathering and preparing materials, running experiments, analyzing data, interpreting findings; real, important contributions to the field of linguistics via replication studies (as mandated by the scientific method).
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Linguistics, with a Major CGPA of 9.0, and permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Honours Thesis in Linguistics
courses.description: A thesis project selected in consultation with the School and carried out under the direction of a faculty supervisor.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Linguistics with a CGPA of 10.0 in the major; one of LING 3004 , LING 3007 , LING 3505 , or LING 3601 ; and permission of the instructor.

free_electives.text: BIOL (Biology) except BIOL 4810 which may be used only as a free elective for any B.Sc. program. Biochemistry students may use BIOL 2005 only as a free elective.

ERTH (Earth Sciences), except ERTH 2415 which may be used only as a free elective for any B.Sc. program. Students in Earth Sciences programs may use ERTH 2401 , ERTH 2402 , and ERTH 2403 only as free electives.

TSES (Technology, Society, Environment) except TSES 2305 . Biology students may use these courses only as free electives. Integrated Science and Environmental Science students may include these courses in their programs but may not count them as part of the Science Sequence.

TSES (Technology, Society, Environment)  Biology students may use these courses only as free electives.

Any course is allowable as a Free Elective providing it is not prohibited (see below). Students are expected to comply with prerequisite requirements and enrolment restrictions for all courses as published in this Calendar.

Advanced Science Faculty Electives are courses at the 2000-4000 level chosen from the Science Faculty Electives list above.

All courses offered by the Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences, the Faculty of Public and Global Affairs, and the Sprott School of Business are approved as Arts or Social Sciences courses EXCEPT FOR: All Science Geography courses (see list above), all Geomatics (GEOM) courses, all Science Psychology courses (see list above). ISAP 1000 may be used as an Approved Course Outside the Faculties of Science and Engineering and Design.

Any course is allowable as a Free Elective providing it is not prohibited (see below). Students are expected to comply with prerequisite requirements and enrolment restrictions for all courses as published in this Calendar.
